input_format: nv12 at runtime, rgb at train/calibration
outputs: 3 raw detection heads [1,18,{80,40,20},{80,40,20}] + drivable-area seg [1,2,640,640] + lane-line seg [1,2,640,640]
licence: YOLOP upstream terms not yet reviewed — check hustvl/YOLOP before redistribution

toolchain.note: The parent ONNX is downloaded, not re-exported (byte-identical to the upstream release asset). export.py only cuts the anchor decode.

cosine.note: Cosine is measured per output tensor. The detection heads are raw conv outputs; the real acceptance for detection is boxes-after-decode, which is a BCDL-side check, not a cosine on the head tensor.

the_cut.why: The uncut graph bakes the anchor decode with ScatterND. It compiles WITHOUT ERROR and the objectness/class columns of the detection output are never written — every box comes back zero-confidence. This is a silent, total detector failure on a clean compile.
the_cut.fix: Cut the graph before the decode; emit the 3 raw detection heads and decode on the CPU in BCDL. The two segmentation heads are unaffected and pass straight through.
the_cut.general_lesson: When a head's decode does not survive quantised lowering, keep the conv trunk on the BPU and move the postprocess arithmetic to the host.

measured.note: Not re-measured in this repo. The shipped build is board-validated in BCDL's panoptic-driving example; latency/accuracy live in BCDL's benchmark results, not here.

status: Recipe reconstructs the cut from the known YOLOP graph structure (three 18-channel detection-head convs at strides 8/16/32, discovered by shape). export.py has been written to survive a re-download of the release ONNX; --det-heads overrides the discovery if a future release renames tensors. Not re-run through the toolchain in this repo.
